title: Release practices policy | Stats NZ
content:
## Release practices policy
10 June 2021, 2:00pm
The Government Statistician is responsible and accountable for the release of data, statistics, and information produced by Stats NZ.
This policy for release practices guides how we give access to our data, statistics, and information.
We release data, official statistics, insights, and information in a transparent and well-managed manner in order to:
- maintain people's trust in the data, Stats NZ, and New Zealand's data system
- ensure our customers, stakeholders, and commentators are well informed
- promote the value of the data and its story
- be in accordance with Te Tiriti o Waitangi and relationship agreements, to work with iwi and Māori to identify opportunities for reflecting Te Ao Māori in our products and services.
The following five principles must be applied when considering the appropriate practice for a release:
- Independence and impartiality : The statutory independence and impartiality of the Government Statistician is ensured.
- Integrity : Objectivity and professionalism are maintained.
- Accessibility : Access is equal and open to all.
- Reliability : The processes used to deliver data, statistics, and information are applied consistently.
- Timeliness : Release dates are signalled in advance, and results are disseminated as soon as practicable after they have been compiled.
This policy applies to releases produced by Stats NZ alone or in collaboration with other agencies as well as iwi and Māori and is irrespective of funding sources.

## Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ's Te Tiriti obligations
Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ's Te Tiriti obligations arise from Te Tiriti o Waitangi/the Treaty of Waitangi, the Public Service Act 2020 and the Data and Statistics Act 2022, the latter of which specifically sets out the expectation upon Tatauranga Aotearoa Stats NZ, to recognise and respect the Crown's responsibility to give effect to the principles of Te Tiriti o Waitangi/ the Treaty of Waitangi.
Details of release practices are available in:
- Information releases, embargoes, and sanctions
- Mana Ōrite Relationship Agreement
This policy and associated procedures and standards are guided by:
- Data and Statistics Act 2022
- United Nations Fundamental Principles of Official Statistics
- The Principles and Protocols for Producers of Tier 1 Statistics
- The Privacy Act 2020
- The Public Service Act 2020
- Microdata access guidelines
- Ngā Tikanga Paihere framework
Policy owner: Rachael Milicich, Deputy Chief Executive/Deputy Government Statistician, Insights & Statistics
Responsible person: General Manager, Customer Services & Support
Effective date: 31 March 2026
Date of next review: March 2028
